tagline: A community that helps each other — donations, support, resources and human connections.

hero.subtitle: A social network connecting people who need help with people who want to help. Simple, transparent and from the heart.

vision.subtitle: The capitalist kibbutz
vision.paragraph1Emphasis: Digital kibbutz in a capitalist world.
vision.paragraph1Rest: A social network for unity, centralization, accessibility and distribution of philanthropy and social action of all kinds for everyone.
vision.paragraph2Emphasis: We are currently in the process of setting up.
vision.paragraph2Rest: Everything written below is part of the vision we want to build, and we invite you to help us build it.
vision.paragraph3: We invite you to join us to change the world and create real social change. To be part of something big, moral at the highest level, technological, social and many more good things.
vision.paragraph4: KarmaCommunity is more than a social network — it is a movement that connects people who want to do good, with no commercial interests, no ads, just real humanity and community.
vision.motto2: "Everyone has something they need and something they would be happy to give"

1.title: Mutual aid
1.text: Posts of requests/offers to help in any area: food, furniture, time, knowledge and more.

2.title: Chats and community
2.text: Private and group chat, topic discussions, notifications for what matters to you.

3.title: Proximity and discovery
3.text: Search by location and interests, tailored recommendations, follow orgs and people.

4.title: Community organizations
4.text: Organizations have a dashboard, volunteers/resources management and approval flows.

paragraph.1: I'm a product developer who believes in the power of community. KarmaCommunity was born to make good more accessible —
paragraph.2: to connect those in need with those who can help, transparently and simply. Happy to chat if you'd like to hear more.

paragraph.1: Karma Community (KC) presents a non-profit social network, combining capitalist efficiency with humanity and collaboration.
paragraph.2: A digital space enabling everyone to give and receive — time, money, items and knowledge — with a feed full of community action.
paragraph.3: Our motto: Giving is also receiving.

bullet.1: Duplication — wasted resources and unnecessary competition
bullet.2: Trust — standardization and transparency for donations and initiatives
bullet.3: Community — building a soft and strong network in parallel

solution.title: The solution
solution.text: A unified, easy-to-use, transparent app — surfaces nearby giving/getting opportunities, connects people and orgs, and builds trust and cooperation.

1.title: Join
1.text: Sign up quickly, choose language and interests.

2.title: Discover
2.text: Find requests/offers by location, topic and community.

3.title: Act
3.text: Connect, help, share — and the community grows.

individuals.title: Individuals
individuals.text: Sharing items, food donations, study help, small repairs, rides, emotional support, community discussion and more.

orgs.title: Organizations
orgs.text: Unified dashboard for requests, volunteers and resources; trust and transparency; approval flows.

values.title: Our values
values.subtitle: No ads, no harmful content, strong community

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: We'd love to chat, collaborate or welcome you

faq.a1: Yes. It's a community initiative aiming to remain accessible to everyone.
faq.a2: Join as a user, join groups, or reach out for collaborations.
faq.q3: Is the app available for Android/iOS?
faq.a3: Yes. We have Android and iOS versions, and a convenient web version.

1.text: I found a volunteer who fixed my door in a day! Amazing to have a community that helps so fast.

2.text: We donated furniture through the app to a local family. It was simple and transparent.

3.text: I found free math tutoring for an elderly neighbor. A wonderful connection formed.

partners.title: Partners and communities
partners.subtitle: On the way, in action, by our side

point.3: Privacy and respect for every user

step.1: Fill a form — basic details, goals and needs.
step.2: Short verification — transparency check, documents and contact details.
step.3: Launch — dashboard, posts, volunteers and resources.

paragraph.1: In an era of noise, ads and interests — we choose community. A community that values action, solidarity and good spirit.
paragraph.2: Together we can connect needs to abilities, hearts to hands. Good is infrastructure, not a product.
paragraph.3: We commit to transparency, human dignity and a safe digital space. Together we build infrastructure that empowers community action — everywhere.

moreTestimonials.title: More stories from the field
moreTestimonials.item1: I met neighbors through KC, and suddenly there's a building community. Small things that make change. — Ilan, Petah Tikva
moreTestimonials.item2: As a small org we got massive exposure to quality volunteers. The dashboard saved us hours. — Yad LaLev NGO
moreTestimonials.item3: Gave a few hours a week, got a huge sense of meaning and new friends. — Sivan, Ramat Gan

moreFaq.title: More questions
moreFaq.a1: We minimize data collection and give users control over visibility and exposure.
moreFaq.a2: No. The platform is ad-free and focuses solely on community content.
moreFaq.q3: How do I report an issue?
moreFaq.a3: There's a built-in reporting mechanism on every post/chat. You can also contact us directly via WhatsApp.

problems.title: The problems we're here to solve
problems.subtitle: Why do we need KC at all?
problems.card1Title: Duplication, fragmentation and lack of trust
problems.card1Text: Today there are so many platforms, WhatsApp groups and nonprofits trying to help. Everyone works alone, there's duplication, lack of coordination, and it's hard to know who to trust. Karma Community unites all of this in one place, transparent and reliable.
problems.card2Title: The dissonance between community and freedom
problems.card2Text: For years people have felt the dissonance between the desire for community and the desire for freedom. Every community has its own limits and norms. Karma Community offers a platform, a kind of social network, that emphasizes both togetherness and freedom and openness.
problems.card3Text: In an era of social networks driven by money and ads, we see the real potential and need we have as humans for these "networks". Karma Community offers a social network with no ads and no harmful content. A platform that celebrates community and sharing.

features.sectionTitle: Everything you need to do good
features.sectionSubtitle: Simple tools that make mutual aid part of everyday life
features.feature1Text: Easily post a request or offer and get a response from the community around you. From tutoring to small repairs at home.
features.feature2Text: Find interest groups, join discussions, and create new connections with people who care.
features.feature3Text: Our smart map will show you where you're needed, right near home.
features.feature4Text: No ads, no harmful content, just real community and mutual trust.

howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.subtitle: A simple, clear process that connects people
howItWorks.intro: Karma Community is built on a simple principle: everyone can give and everyone can receive. Our process is designed to be simple, transparent and friendly.
howItWorks.step1Text: Sign up in seconds — just basic details. Tell us what interests you, where you are, and what you can offer or need. No unnecessary info, only what matters.
howItWorks.step2Text: Need help? Post a clear request with what you need, when and where. Want to help? Post an offer with what you can give. Or just scroll the feed and see what's happening around you.
howItWorks.step3Text: Our smart map will show you where you're needed, right near home. Your personal feed will show you relevant requests and offers by location and interest.
howItWorks.step4Text: Saw something that interests you? Send a direct message, coordinate details, meet the person behind the request or offer. Everything is transparent, safe and simple.
howItWorks.step5Title: Do good and feel the difference
howItWorks.step5Text: Coordinate, meet, help or get help. Every action like this creates a real human connection and strengthens the community. You'll see your impact, and the community will see your contribution.
howItWorks.note: Everything is free, everything is transparent, everything for the community. No bureaucracy, no fees, just people helping people.

who.title: Who is it for?
who.subtitle: Everyone. Really.
who.introRest: Rich and poor, center and periphery — regardless of religion, race, gender, nationality or even physical location. Everyone can give and everyone can receive.
who.intro: Karma Community is for everyone. Rich and poor, center and periphery — regardless of religion, race, gender, nationality or even physical location. Everyone can give and everyone can receive.
who.individualsText: In the early stages, KarmaCommunity focuses on individuals — neighbors, friends, and anyone who wants to give their time, knowledge or belongings to help others.
who.bullet5: Everyone is welcome to volunteer and contribute to the community itself and be part of the founders
who.orgsText: In the next stages we'll connect nonprofits and organizations with dedicated tools for managing volunteers, donations and requests. Important — this will only happen after we establish a strong community of individuals.

values.title: The values that guide us
values.subtitle: What makes Karma Community a safe and trusted community
values.intro: We lead change through a system that puts people at the center. Every feature is evaluated by its contribution to transparency, human connections and the ability to measure real impact.
values.commitment1: User and org verification before launch
values.commitment3: Sustainable growth — no ads, no excess data

coreMottos.title: Our guiding principles
coreMottos.subtitle: What drives us forward
coreMottos.motto1: One person's leftovers can be another's treasure
coreMottos.motto2: Each of us has something to give and something we'd be happy to receive
coreMottos.motto3: To give is also to receive

roadmap.title: Our roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: The plan to expand the community's impact
roadmap.step1Label: First app release for Android and iOS
roadmap.step2Label: Advanced features for sharing info with other nonprofits and WhatsApp/Facebook groups
roadmap.step3Label: Partnerships with municipalities and welfare agencies
roadmap.logoEvolutionSubtitle: Our logo went through several versions to get here
roadmap.brandStrip: Moving forward with the community — every piece of feedback affects our priorities.

about.title: One community. One goal.
about.subtitle: The story of Karma Community
about.para1: In a world full of noise, we believe in the quiet power of doing good. Karma Community was born from a simple need: to connect people. Between those who need help and those who can and want to lend a hand. We saw the duplication, the lack of trust and the scattered efforts, and decided to create one platform that unites everyone.
about.para2: No ads, no interests — just technology in the service of humanity. Our mission is to make giving a natural, simple part of everyone's daily life, and to create a more connected, supportive and caring Israeli society.
about.founderSubtitle: A word from the founder, Nave Sarussi
about.founderPara1: From a young age I felt privileged and that my life was in order. In the army, despite reaching a good position as an aircraft programmer, I didn't feel the meaning I was looking for. I always thought about how I could bring real, positive change to the world. I was always interested in working at scale and positively impacting as many people as possible.
about.founderPara2: Karma Community is my way to make good more accessible, to create a platform that connects people who want to help with people who need help. I believe in the power of community to change reality, and I'd be happy for you to join me on this journey.
about.githubDescription: This is the app's code. Everyone is welcome to look and contribute.

partners.title: Together we create change
partners.subtitle: Proud to partner with organizations that share our vision

instagram.title: Follow us on Instagram
instagram.subtitle: Photos and videos from our community

faq.title: Q&A
faq.subtitle: Everything you wanted to know about Karma Community
faq.q1: Does using the app cost money?
faq.a1: No. Karma Community is a nonprofit initiative, and using it will always be free for everyone. We believe mutual aid should be accessible to everyone, at no cost.
faq.q2: How can I donate or volunteer?
faq.a2: The best way is to download the app, join the community and start responding to requests. You can also always contact us directly via WhatsApp, email or our WhatsApp group. Every bit of help is appreciated!
faq.q3: Is the app available for both Android and iOS?
faq.a3: The app is currently under development. Meanwhile, we have a web site you can use from any browser — also under ongoing development. We're working on Android and iOS versions to launch in the future.
faq.a4: Absolutely not. The platform is completely ad-free and focuses only on community content. We're committed to a clean, community-focused experience with no commercial distractions.
faq.q5: How do I start using the app?
faq.a5: Very simple! Download the app, sign up with a few basic details (or use guest mode), and you can immediately start posting requests or offers, or scroll and discover what's happening in your community.
faq.a6: That's our vision — to create a digital community that works like a kibbutz: sharing, mutual aid, shared responsibility, but in the modern digital world. Everyone can give and everyone can receive, with no bureaucracy and no fees.
faq.q7: How do you ensure security and privacy?
faq.a7: We take security and privacy very seriously. All data is encrypted, we don't share information with third parties, and we have a user verification system. Users can also control their privacy and decide what to share.
faq.q8: What's the difference between Karma Community and other platforms?
faq.a8: The main difference is that we're nonprofit, ad-free, and 100% focused on community and mutual aid. We don't sell data, we don't try to make money from users, and everything we do is for the community.
faq.q9: How can I help develop the app?
faq.a9: We're always happy for help! You can help with code (everything is open on GitHub), design, content, testing, or simply be part of the community and share feedback. Contact us via WhatsApp or email and we'll be happy to tell you how you can help.
faq.q10: What if I run into a technical issue?
faq.a10: If you've hit a technical problem, we're here to help! Contact us via WhatsApp, email, or our WhatsApp group. We'll do our best to respond as soon as possible and resolve the issue. Your feedback matters a lot to us!
